question: Implement a `StringWrapper` class that dynamically allocates memory for a C-style string. Provide a copy constructor that performs a deep copy to prevent issues with shallow copies.

test_cases.input: StringWrapper s1("Hello, World!"); StringWrapper s2 = s1; { StringWrapper temp1("First String"); StringWrapper temp2 = temp1; }
test_cases.expected:
--- Test 1: Basic Copy ---
s1 original: Hello, World!
s2 (copy of s1): Hello, World!

--- Test 2: Scope and Deletion (expect crash/UB) ---
temp1: First String
temp2: First String

Program finished (if no crash occurred).

errors: Copy constructor performs a shallow copy instead of a deep copy, leading to double-free or use-after-free when objects are destroyed.
